summary: Removed the repository-hosted Svelte progress site and replaced it with decomp.dev links and a static redirect page. The GitHub Pages generation container no longer installs Node, clones progress assets, fetches progress JSON, or builds the local progress-site app; it now emits /progress/index.html as a redirect to https://decomp.dev/doldecomp/melee. The README badges were updated to use decomp.dev code, data, and linked-code progress shields.
what_changed: Deleted the local Svelte progress-site project under tools/progress-site, including package-lock.json, package.json, Svelte source, styles, tests, TypeScript config, Vite config, Playwright config, eslint/prettier config, .npmrc, .gitignore, and its AGPL license file.
Added tools/progress-redirect.html, a minimal HTML document with a zero-second meta refresh and canonical link pointing to https://decomp.dev/doldecomp/melee.

Changed .github/packages/gen-pages/entrypoint.sh so the pages job creates /output/progress and copies tools/progress-redirect.html to /output/progress/index.html instead of building the Svelte progress site.

Removed gen-pages logic that copied external assets, fetched progress JSON from progress.decomp.club, installed npm dependencies, ran npm run build, and moved the generated build directory into /output/progress.

Simplified .github/packages/gen-pages/setup.sh by removing NodeSource setup, nodejs installation, npm dependency installation, progress asset sparse checkout from doldecomp/assets, and npm cache cleanup.
Updated .github/packages/build-linux/Dockerfile so the gen-pages stage no longer copies tools/progress-site/package*.json into /tmp, and changed several ENTRYPOINT forms to JSON-array exec form.

Updated .github/README.md badges from progress.decomp.club endpoints and the old progress page to decomp.dev shields and decomp.dev project URL. Added a linked-code progress badge using measure=complete_code.
smart_moves: Kept the old /progress/ URL functional by publishing a tiny redirect page instead of simply deleting the hosted path, preserving inbound links to doldecomp.github.io/melee/progress/.
Moved progress reporting to decomp.dev rather than maintaining a repository-specific Svelte app and custom progress-data plumbing.
Removed the Node/npm dependency chain from the gen-pages container, substantially reducing CI/page-generation setup work and dependency surface.
Deleted obsolete generated/frontend tooling wholesale instead of leaving dead Svelte, Vite, npm, lint, and test files behind.
Used reference-style Markdown badge definitions in .github/README.md, making badge targets easier to update and search.
Switched Docker ENTRYPOINT declarations to exec-form arrays, avoiding shell-form entrypoint quirks while touching the Dockerfile for the progress-site removal.
decomp_lessons: When a shared decompilation service can provide progress dashboards and badges, prefer linking to it over maintaining a bespoke per-repo frontend.
For decomp project status pages, preserve legacy URLs with a static redirect when replacing infrastructure; external documentation, Discord messages, and bookmarks may still point at the old path.
CI/page-generation containers should avoid installing large frontend toolchains unless they are still required for the generated artifacts.

Progress badges should point at stable service endpoints and include explicit parameters such as mode=shield, measure=code, measure=data, measure=complete_code, label, and category=all so the README documents exactly what is being measured.
Removing stale tooling can be a decomp productivity improvement even when no game code changes: fewer dependencies and scripts means fewer unrelated build failures.
naming_conventions: decomp.dev badge URLs use the project path form decomp.dev/doldecomp/melee.svg with query parameters for mode, measure, label, and category.
The README labels progress badges as Code Progress, Data Progress, and Linked Progress, with the linked-code badge rendered as label=Linked%20Code and measure=complete_code.
The retained compatibility file is named tools/progress-redirect.html and is copied to /output/progress/index.html to serve the legacy progress route.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or code-matching tactics were present; this PR was infrastructure/documentation cleanup rather than decompilation matching work.

The removed gen-pages script comment documented a previous race condition between build-melee and gen-pages when fetching progress from progress.decomp.club; replacing the local progress-site build avoids that specific progress-data fetch path.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR context.
There is no direct reviewer rationale in the dump; conclusions are based on the title and diff.
follow_up_queries: Find PRs that changed decomp.dev badge URLs or progress metrics.
Find PRs that removed local web tooling or Node dependencies from CI.
Find PRs that preserved old GitHub Pages URLs with redirect HTML.
